title: Vulneraria heterophylla
taxonomy: Vulneraria heterophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Vulneraria, which is a small group of flowering plants native to Europe. The family Lamiaceae, to which Vulneraria heterophylla belongs, is a large and diverse family of plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle family. Vulneraria heterophylla is classified as a perennial herb, and its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. The specific classification of Vulneraria heterophylla reflects its evolutionary relationships and phylogenetic position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Vulneraria heterophylla, also known as Alchemilla heterophylla, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been employed to treat various ailments, including wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Vulneraria heterophylla is believed to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. The plant has also been used to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Vulneraria heterophylla as a medicinal treatment.

Additionally, Vulneraria heterophylla may have antibacterial properties, which could aid in preventing infections. Further research is needed to confirm the exact health benefits of this plant.

preparations: Vulneraria heterophylla, also known as yellow mountain sorrel, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
